tokens: Tony, I'm stopping a little short (of the end of the cable) on the Heat Flow Electronics because, if I go on out, I'd be in a little crater and I couldn't get it leveled.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Perhaps looking east for Double Spot) That little crater might be...(Pause) That little crater just might be what-you-call-it, you know?
(Pause) I think we may have come a little further than we thought we were going to, Houston.
I see Double Spot back there - or what looks like Double Spot - and we're a good ways past that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, we're not much off, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Passing on a question) John, did you happen to notice what heading you were driving on the way out to the ALSEP site?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I wasn't watching it, to be honest with you.
I was trying to keep sight of old Charlie.
You can get lost out here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, the Temp labels on the RTG package say "nothing".
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, John, getting your alignments up here is gonna be something.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll work that problem, Charlie, when the time comes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, the old idiot-proof decal has made it...The probe is in the left hand, and the wires are not crossed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; the temperature...The shorting plug is pulled and the temperature (means "ampere") reading is reading about like...Golly!
It's reading (chuckles) about like three-quarter scale.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, let me give you a question here.
The down-Sun heat probe is gonna be within 2 meters of about a 5-meter (diameter) crater.
Is that okay, or do you want me to move it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you move it to a crater-free area?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yes, I can; but it'll be more towards the portable (magnetometer)...(Correcting himself) I mean the LSM (Lunar Surface Magnetometer).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'd like to do that.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Now the one up-Sun is perfect, straight up-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, this is a super place right here for this up-Sun one.
Okay, here we go with a big drill coming up!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Down into the crater he goes.
Looks like the big eye is looking at something else (namely, the LM).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, John, I'm gonna need about 23 gallons of water.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that (RTG) connector is made, somehow.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Thinking about what he needs) Okay; drill.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (I'll go) back over here and straighten out the line.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Charlie) Now, while you're standing over at the Rover there, could you read off the heading?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, I almost tried...(Answering Tony) Oh, yeah, bearing to the LM is 033, the heading is 195.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That line between the - see it - the Central Station and the ALSEP (means the RTG) is gonna float in the air.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, things are really stiff, aren't they?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, do you want us to tilt that package...That RTG package is okay with dirt on its floor, isn't it?
Does it need to be all white?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) No, it doesn't have to be all white.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's my first question of the day.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I didn't think it had thermal problems.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Dropping something out of view) I knew I was gonna do that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I see it doesn't stand up any better there than it does here, does it?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And I dropped the core caps.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm getting where I can bend down in that suit, Tony.
When I first started off, I was going head over heels, but now...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (As the rack starts to tip) Look at that stupid thing.
(Pause as he returns to the LMP seat) Okay, one more pin.
(Garbled) Lock the collar, and the drill is loose.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Walking into a little 3-meter crater here, Tony, you can see.
Really dig in when you go into those craters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, that is a rocky place, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Hey, John, will I be in your way right here?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Gesturing at the 5-meter crater) I'm gonna have to drill away from that crater.
Tony, I'm about 4 or 5 meters away, is that okay?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'll start drilling right here, then.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, I don't think...That won't be any problem.
The only thing goes down past there is the LSM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we may have to deploy the LSM out behind the ALSEP anyway to get it from going down in that little crater there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You know, John, you need (only) about two patches of Velcro.
You got about 95 (feet, meaning far more than is needed)...There it comes.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We understand you're going to deploy it (meaning the LSM) almost due west of the RTG?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) Okay, and we need about 30 feet between that heat flow hole and the LSM.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: For a minute there I was worried.
I can stab it into the ground about...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, that beauty is going right in!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Whatever it was, we got through it, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It must have been a rock.
I'm sure the regolith is covered with...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and first, the long stem is in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Help me when you get finished.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, the foot mashie is not gonna work.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Gonna have to use the wrench.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That new wrench is pretty slick.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Talking to a piece of equipment) Why don't you open?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm going out for the ballet when I get back.
You learn another line of work up here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, that was fantastic news about the House passing the bill, Tony.
It really started a great day today for us.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, you can't believe how happy I am that (first drill stem) went in there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look at that beauty go again!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, give it time to clean the flutes.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It may appear that I am leaning on it, Tony; but I guarantee you I am not.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's run into something hard down there.
I can feel the torque; but whatever it is, it's going through it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's probably just some rocks down there in the regolith, Tony.
You know, it looks...I bet it's just like the side of that fresh crater we saw back near the LM.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh boy, thank goodness for that wrench.
(Pause) Never would be able to do that with the foot mashie.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Leveling the Passive Seismic Experiment) Almost got it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How can you get that thing leveled out there?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, it's level; but the Sun (compass) reading is 064-1/2.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I pointed it at that thing that said "Sun", but that sure didn't do it.
I guess you can handle that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, it bogs down as it goes down through rocks and things.
It's giving me a lot of torque.
Just...The third stem is just about in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston; switch 5 (on the base of the Central Station) is clockwise.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'll call it quits on that one, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, we understand you have the skirt out (around the PSE)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Can't you see it on the TV?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're just now coming around there.
Okay, that 064 will be fine, there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It (meaning the thermal skirt) ought to blind you (with reflected sunlight).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, do you remember where the bubble was on the top of the PSE?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Gary (Latham, the PSE Principal Investigator) said we couldn't come back unless we put the bubble in the middle.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Oh, I was afraid of this.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This thing pulled so hard that it pulled the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That was my problem with the RTG package, I...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Just that first bunch that we didn't get.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Okay, Tony, I now have the right-handed rammer.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And Tony, Mark (Langseth) has his first one - all the way in to the red mark (on the rammer) - on the Cayley Plain.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The first one in the highlands.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Ask him what we're going to do if the temperature shows like it does at Hadley.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, (on) the second one: the thermal cover is in to the second red mark.
And, Tony, the probe is out of the ground up to B-8.
Right on the line between B-7 and B-8.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Brief Pause) Here's a line that pulled loose.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't know how it happened.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Walking toward the Central Station) Pulled loose from there?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I didn't even know...I didn't even know it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did the wire or the connector come off?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (lost under Tony) had our first catastrophe.
It's broke right at the connector.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The wire came off at the connector.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I guess we can forget the rest of that heat flow.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I'll go do the (deep core).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A bunch of spaghetti over there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, we can sure see that on TV.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Is there no way we can recover from that, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll go over and get the...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We understand that the cable came off the connector, and we've got just the free end of the cable, is that right?
transcript.speaker: England
